candidate.description: Pattern that publishes events atomically with database writes by inserting them into an outbox table within the same DB transaction; a relay process then ships them to a broker.

candidates_full.full_description: Data structures accelerating data retrieval; unrelated to event publishing semantics.
Asynchronous decoupling of producers and consumers via durable queues; outbox patterns commonly publish to these brokers.
Transactions spanning multiple services or databases; transactional outbox is a canonical alternative to 2PC for cross-service consistency.
